summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=2 (raw=2, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: The callback_url is still fully user-controlled and only checked for http/https scheme prefix. An attacker can still target internal IPs or services (e.g. http://localhost:8080/admin). The scheme check does not prevent SSRF against internal hosts.

raw.summary: The code adds a scheme check (http/https) before the urlopen call, which mitigates some SSRF risk (e.g. file://) but does not prevent SSRF attacks against internal hosts (e.g. http://169.254.169.254). The SAST findings are still valid as the full URL is user-controlled and the scheme-only check is insufficient to prevent SSRF. The code otherwise meets functional requirements.

sast_review.reason: The callback_url is still fully user-controlled and only checked for http/https scheme prefix. An attacker can still target internal IPs or services (e.g. http://localhost:8080/admin). The scheme check does not prevent SSRF against internal hosts.

The urlopen call uses a user-controlled URL. The scheme check (http/https) reduces risk from file:// or custom schemes but does not eliminate SSRF risk. This is the same underlying vulnerability as the codeql finding (line 48).
